essence: Evaluates how proposed developments affect the environment and guides decisions to minimise harm and support sustainability.

identity.title: Environmental Impact Assessor
identity.summary: Conducts Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs), analyses ecological and environmental risks, and advises developers and authorities on mitigation and sustainable design.

salary.notes: Senior assessors working on major infrastructure or national projects can exceed £75k-£90k+.

qualifications: Training in environmental science, planning, or sustainability
Knowledge of EIA regulations and planning policy
Ability to analyse environmental risks and impacts
Understanding of ecology, pollution, land use, and mitigation strategies

lifestyle.description: Environmental impact assessors work for consultancies, councils, environmental agencies, and infrastructure projects. They evaluate development proposals, analyse environmental risks, and produce EIA reports that shape planning decisions. The role suits individuals who enjoy analysis, sustainability, policy, and balancing development with environmental protection.

habits: Analyses ecological, social, and environmental risks
Prepares detailed EIA reports and mitigation plans
Engages with stakeholders and environmental bodies
